mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a robust labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS month-over-month growth is consistently positive but S&P 500 20-day returns are predominantly negative (e.g., < 40% positive days) over any 12-month period.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation accelerates significantly, it signals potential monetary tightening by the Fed, which increases discount rates and negatively impacts equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where the CPI trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: A recovery in housing starts, coupled with stable or declining unemployment, indicates improving consumer confidence and economic activity, which positively impacts broad market sentiment and S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month average growth > 5% AND UNRATE 3-month average change < 0
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 60% of periods where both HOUST growth and UNRATE decline conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: A significant deceleration in industrial production, particularly during periods of high interest rates, signals weakening manufacturing and potential recession, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If INDPRO contraction during high FEDFUNDS periods does not lead to significantly lower S&P 500 returns compared to periods of INDPRO contraction with low FEDFUNDS (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and profits, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If the correlation between RSAFS month-over-month growth and S&P 500 5-day forward returns is consistently negative over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market and broader economic weakness, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by > 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances following a trigger event.

mechanism_narrative: A significant rebound in real GDP growth after a period of contraction signals an economic recovery, boosting investor confidence and corporate earnings expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth > 2% after a period of negative growth
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of periods where this trigger condition is met during a post-recessionary phase.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is actively increasing while inflation remains elevated, it indicates aggressive monetary tightening, which typically dampens economic activity and equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS 3-month change > 0.25% AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth > 3%
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL conditions are met.
